title: Programmatic Pixel Art Sprites — Replace placeholders with pixel-art characters and furniture
archive_reason: Superseded by palette-swap approach: HSL color remapping from char_0.png source preserves all shading/highlight detail. Hand-coded pixel arrays replaced by pre-rendered PNGs (char_0..11.png) generated via scripts/export-characters.ts.
description: Replace text letters (A, S, M) and emojis with programmatic pixel-art sprites. Character sprites as 16x24 pixel arrays with per-agent color palettes. Furniture drawn with procedural Canvas 2D paths. Floor and wall tiles get subtle textures. All rendering through TILE_RENDERERS dispatch table. No external assets.

in: Character sprites: 16x24 pixel-art figures for all 5 agents + generic fallback
Per-agent color palettes: Alex=blue, Sarah=purple, Morgan=green, Marcus=orange, Priya=pink, generic=gray
Offscreen canvas sprite caching at 1x scale
Furniture sprites: desk, ceo-desk, conference, whiteboard, mailbox, bell, server-room, door
Floor texture: subtle grid/grout pattern via cached CanvasPattern
Wall texture: baseboard stripe or panel lines
All rendering through TILE_RENDERERS dispatch table
out: H/K/S/P palette abstraction layer (deferred until runtime customization needed)

dod.criterion: All 5 named agents render as pixel-art sprites with distinct color schemes
All furniture tile types render with procedural Canvas path shapes
Floor and wall tiles display subtle textures
No external asset files (PNG, SVG) — all sprites are programmatic
Zoom, pan, click, hover from Project 1 remain functional
npx tsc --noEmit && npx vite build pass with zero errors
All 5+ agents render as 16x24 pixel-art sprites
Each agent has visually distinct color scheme
Sprite rendering wired into TILE_RENDERERS dispatch table
No external assets — all sprites as pixel arrays in TypeScript
All furniture tile types render with procedural Canvas path shapes
All renderers wired into TILE_RENDERERS dispatch table
No external assets — all drawing is programmatic
